reminder.notes:
Cut battery drain on the no-SIM/Wi-Fi location fallback

The location-reminder GPS fallback used when a phone has no SIM and no Wi-Fi
(so Play Services geofencing can't run) was taking a high-accuracy GPS fix on a
fixed timer regardless of whether the phone was actually moving -- a device
sitting still all day still burned GPS every 20s-5min. It is now motion-gated:
when every target is farther than 1.5km away it stops polling GPS entirely and
arms the hardware significant-motion sensor (near-zero power) plus a 20-minute
safety poll, so a stationary phone uses almost no battery until it actually
moves. Close to a geofence boundary it keeps tight, distance-adaptive polling
(30/60/120s) so crossings are still caught on time.

### Features

- No user-facing feature changes. Adds an internal, emulator-driven screenshot
  automation harness (integration_test + scripts/screenshots.sh) used to keep
  the app-store hub visuals in sync; it is not part of the shipped app.

### Bug fixes

- Location reminders no longer drain the battery while the phone is stationary
  with no SIM and no Wi-Fi. The GPS-polling fallback is now gated on the
  hardware motion sensor and only polls when the device is moving or near a
  target, instead of on a fixed high-accuracy timer.

ai-scanner.notes:
# AI Scanner 1.4.0 — search gets its own page

The app now has three pages instead of one. Search is just search, so an answer is never buried under
your whole library. Everything you have scanned lives on a new Library page as a grid of thumbnails,
and the automatic Groups finally have a place of their own instead of hiding in Settings.

### Features
- Search page holds only the search box and its results, with example questions before you type.
- New Library page shows every screenshot as a thumbnail grid, which is far quicker to skim than the
  old list of wide text cards.
- Groups (screenshots grouped automatically by content) moved out of Settings onto its own page.
- A bottom bar switches between Search, Library and Groups.

### Bug fixes
- The AI answer no longer competes for space with the full screenshot list on the same page.
- Scan progress, the photo-permission prompt and pull-to-refresh follow the page they belong to,
  instead of stacking up on a single crowded screen.

mirrordrive.notes:
# MirrorDrive 0.13.2

### Features
- No new features. This release fixes the in-app updater and one playback behavior.

### Bug fixes
- The in-app update check now actually works on a real phone. It previously gave up after 8 seconds while the network request itself could take up to 20, so on a normal mobile connection the check was abandoned before it finished and the update prompt never appeared. The check is now non-blocking: the app opens immediately and the update screen appears as soon as a new version is found.
- Deleting a file that is currently playing now stops playback at the same time, instead of continuing to play a file that no longer exists.

twinclean.notes: Diagnostic fix: the 'Connect Google Drive' failure path now shows the real underlying error instead of a generic message, so sign-in issues can actually be diagnosed. No feature changes.

localsender.notes:
Local Sender 1.0.9 - Google Drive backup fixed on Android

Backup now uses the native Google sign-in on phones instead of a browser. No
messaging or pairing changes; pairs with 1.0.7 and later.

### Features
- On Android and iPhone, Google Drive backup now signs in with the native Google
  account picker - no browser, no localhost page.

### Bug fixes
- Android backup no longer opens a browser that returns to a "localhost" page
  that could not complete the sign-in. Desktop backup is unchanged.
